summary: Matched two Push On stage/ground functions: grPushOn_802182C8 and fn_80218678. The PR replaced placeholder decomp markers with C implementations, added the needed mp/mplib.h include for mpLib_80057BC0, and tightened fn_80218678's header prototype from unknown return/params to bool fn_80218678(void). The PR body reports a clean ninja build and 100.0 fuzzy match percent for all functions.
what_changed: Implemented grPushOn_802182C8, which initializes grPushOn_804D6AB8 from Ground_801C49F8(), updates stage_info.unk8C.b4/b5, spawns or initializes entries via grPushOn_802183E4(0) and grPushOn_802183E4(1), then calls mpLib_80057BC0(0).

Implemented fn_80218678 as a bool-returning helper that fetches Ground_801C2BA4(1), null-checks the returned HSD_GObj and its user_data Ground pointer, and returns true only when gp->u.map.xC4_b0 is set.
Added #include "mp/mplib.h" to grpushon.c to support the mpLib_80057BC0 call.
Updated grpushon.h prototype for fn_80218678 from UNK_RET fn_80218678(UNK_PARAMS) to bool fn_80218678(void).
smart_moves: Converted an unknown placeholder prototype to a concrete bool(void) signature only after the implementation made the return behavior and lack of parameters evident.
Preserved defensive null checks around Ground_801C2BA4(1), gobj->user_data, and the map flag access, matching the control flow implied by the decompiled function.
Used existing stage/ground helpers and fields rather than inventing new abstractions: Ground_801C49F8, grPushOn_802183E4, Ground_801C2BA4, stage_info.unk8C, and Ground::u.map.xC4_b0.
decomp_lessons: Small stage functions can often be matched by expressing straightforward initialization sequences directly and keeping call order exact.
For bool flag-check helpers, explicit nested null checks can be important for matching and for safely reaching deep struct fields such as gobj->user_data->u.map.xC4_b0.
When a formerly unknown function is proven to be a no-argument bool helper, update the header prototype in the same PR so callsites and future decompilation work benefit from the recovered type.
If a matched function introduces a library dependency, add the narrow header include needed for that symbol rather than relying on indirect declarations.
naming_conventions: Kept existing address-based names for unresolved stage functions: grPushOn_802182C8, fn_80218678, grPushOn_802183E4.
Used project type names HSD_GObj, Ground_GObj, and Ground consistently.
Retained anonymous/unknown field names such as stage_info.unk8C.b4, stage_info.unk8C.b5, and gp->u.map.xC4_b0 where no stronger semantic names were evidenced.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Replaced `/// #function` markers with direct C implementations for two functions.
Matched grPushOn_802182C8 by preserving a simple sequence of global assignment, bitfield writes, two indexed setup calls, and an mpLib call.
Matched fn_80218678 with explicit NULL comparisons and early true only on a specific Ground map bitfield.
Verification evidence from the PR body: `ninja` builds clean and `fuzzy_match_percent: 100.0` for all functions.
follow_up_queries: Look for other grpushon.c placeholder functions near grPushOn_802186C8, grPushOn_802187A4, and grPushOn_802187A8 that may use the same Ground_GObj and Ground::u.map patterns.
Search for other stage modules that call Ground_801C2BA4(index) and test gp->u.map.xC4_b0 to infer a better semantic name for xC4_b0.
Compare other stage init functions that set stage_info.unk8C.b4/b5 and call mpLib_80057BC0(0) to understand the shared initialization convention.
